exampleSentencesTranslated: I don't want to force you to do something you don't like.
Parents usually do not force their children to choose a career.
Friends should not force each other to drink alcohol at the party.
notes_md:
**ép** = to force someone to do something they don’t want to do. It can also mean to press or squeeze something to get liquid out.

As a verb, it’s often about pressure — emotional, social, or physical.  

It’s also used for physical actions like squeezing fruit or pressing something flat.  
Close synonym: **buộc** (stronger, more formal “force”).
notes:
ép = to force someone to do something they don’t want to do. It can also mean to press or squeeze something to get liquid out.

As a verb, it’s often about pressure — emotional, social, or physical.  

It’s also used for physical actions like squeezing fruit or pressing something flat.  
Close synonym: buộc (stronger, more formal “force”).
exampleSentences: Anh không muốn ép em làm điều gì em không thích.
Ba mẹ thường không ép con cái chọn nghề nghiệp.
Bạn bè không nên ép nhau uống rượu trong buổi tiệc.

example.es: Voy a exprimir jugo de naranja para el desayuno.
example.en: I will squeeze orange juice for breakfast.
example.vi: Tôi sẽ ép nước cam cho bữa sáng.

definitions.en: To press or squeeze something to extract juice or liquid.
definitions.vi: Ép là hành động ấn hoặc bóp một cái gì đó để lấy nước hoặc chất lỏng.
definitions.es: Presionar o exprimir algo para extraer jugo o líquido.
